text.title: The Mom Test Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Script asks 'Would you pay $19/mo for this?' — prospect says 'sure' to be polite.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused interest signal with commitment signal.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite as 'How much are you currently paying for X?' or 'Walk me through the last time you tried to solve this.'

antipattern.symptom: Recorded transcript shows interviewer talking 45% of the time; mostly explaining the product.
antipattern.root-cause: Excitement to share the idea overrode discovery discipline.
antipattern.fix: Use signal_classifier_output.interviewer_word_share as a hard ceiling at 0.20; abort and reschedule if exceeded.

antipattern.symptom: Founder reports interview as positive because 5 of 7 prospects said 'sounds great' — no current-spend evidence.
antipattern.fix: Require signal_classifier_output.commitment_count > 0 before any roadmap commitment.

antipattern.symptom: Script omits 'who would you NOT recommend this to' — only positive framing.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wanted enthusiasm, not honest scope.
antipattern.fix: Add the anti-question to the script; it disarms generic praise and surfaces real constraints.
